summary: This document outlines REST-style API specifications for the NPCI Unified Layer, a central interoperable platform designed to standardize communication and information sharing between government agencies and participant banks. It facilitates Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) and welfare schemes by enabling precise control over fund distribution using Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) programmability. The APIs support beneficiary data management, scheme rule definition, and fund unlocking/locking mechanisms, aiming to reduce integration complexity and overhead for participating entities.

apis.name: Beneficiary Management (Government)
apis.description: APIs for Government agencies to upload and manage DBT beneficiary details in the Unified Layer, including initial data submission and subsequent status updates.

operations.summary: Upload new DBT beneficiary details to the Unified Layer for eligibility checks.

bodySchema.description: Beneficiary data to be stored. Unified Layer will assign a UUID.

200.description: Beneficiary details successfully uploaded and a UUID assigned.

operations.summary: Update the eligibility status (active/inactive) of an existing DBT beneficiary record.

pathParams.name: uuid
pathParams.description: Unique identifier for the beneficiary record, assigned by the Unified Layer.

errors.description: Invalid beneficiary UUID or status value.

apis.name: Beneficiary Query (Bank)
apis.description: APIs for Banks to query the Unified Layer to confirm beneficiary eligibility for specific CBDC schemes.

operations.summary: Query the Unified Layer to confirm beneficiary eligibility using various identifiers. At least one identifier is mandatory.

queryParams.name: beneficiary_aadhaar_hash
queryParams.description: SHA256 hashed Aadhaar number of the beneficiary.

queryParams.name: other_id_type
queryParams.description: Type of other identification (e.g., PAN, Passport).

bodySchema.description: No request body for GET operation.

errors.description: Invalid request parameters or no identifier provided.
Beneficiary record not found for the provided identifiers.

apis.name: Scheme Management (Government)
apis.description: APIs for Government agencies to manage DBT scheme-related information in the Unified Layer, including creation, modification, and rule ID deactivation.

operations.summary: Upload new DBT scheme details to the Unified Layer. A unique combination of scheme_code and start_date identifies a scheme version.

bodySchema.description: Scheme parameters to be stored. Unified Layer will assign a UUID.

200.description: Scheme details successfully uploaded and a UUID assigned.

errors.description: Invalid input data or duplicate scheme_code and start_date combination.

operations.summary: Modify existing DBT scheme details based on the scheme's unique identifier (UUID).

pathParams.name: uuid
pathParams.description: Unique identifier for the scheme record, assigned by the Unified Layer.

bodySchema.description: Complete updated scheme parameters. This operation replaces the existing record.

errors.description: Scheme record not found for the provided UUID.

operations.summary: Deactivate a Rule ID associated with a scheme record by updating its status.

pathParams.name: uuid
pathParams.description: Unique identifier for the scheme record, assigned by the Unified Layer.

bodySchema.description: Request to mark the Rule ID as inactive. 'type' field indicates deletion request.

200.description: Rule ID status successfully updated to Inactive.

bodySchema.message: Rule ID status updated to inactive.

errors.description: Invalid request or missing required fields.
Scheme record not found for the provided UUID.

apis.name: Scheme Query (Bank)
apis.description: APIs for Banks to retrieve scheme-related information from the Unified Layer to create and enforce CBDC rules.

operations.summary: Fetch scheme construct details from the Unified Layer. If only scheme_code is provided, all versions are returned; otherwise, a unique record for scheme_code + start_date.

queryParams.name: start_date
queryParams.description: Start date of the scheme version (YYYY-MM-DD).

bodySchema.description: No request body for GET operation.

errors.description: Scheme record(s) not found for the provided criteria.

apis.name: Rule ID Query (Government)
apis.description: API for Government agencies to view and verify Rule IDs created or updated by Banks for a given scheme.

operations.summary: Retrieve Rule IDs associated with scheme records for a given scheme code and optional start date.

queryParams.name: start_date
queryParams.description: Start date of the scheme version (YYYY-MM-DD).

bodySchema.description: No request body for GET operation.

errors.description: Scheme record(s) not found for the provided criteria.

apis.name: Rule ID Management (Bank)
apis.description: API for Banks to associate a Rule ID with a scheme record in the Unified Layer, ensuring traceability between scheme parameters and CBDC rules.

operations.summary: Update the Rule ID field and Rule Creator IIN for a specific scheme record in the Unified Layer.

pathParams.name: uuid
pathParams.description: Unique identifier for the scheme record, assigned by the Unified Layer.

bodySchema.description: Rule ID and IIN of the bank creating/updating the rule.

errors.description: Unauthorized to update Rule ID (e.g., Rule ID already set by another bank).
Scheme record not found for the provided UUID.

apis.name: Fund Control (Government)
apis.description: API for Government agencies to trigger fund unlocking or locking requests for beneficiary/merchant wallets.

operations.summary: Initiate a request to unlock or lock beneficiary/merchant funds, routed by the Unified Layer to the relevant banks.

bodySchema.description: Details for the fund unlock/lock request.

200.description: Unlock/Lock request successfully initiated and routed. Response contains status from destination banks.

errors.description: Invalid request parameters or malformed request body.

nonFunctional.security: Highly confidential information, protected by copyright and intellectual property laws. Requires secure API integration, UI with maker-checker controls for file uploads, and SHA256 hashing for sensitive data like Aadhaar numbers. Access is restricted under license agreements.
nonFunctional.observability: Includes mechanisms for checking transaction status (Check Transaction API for timeouts) and system health (Heartbeat API) between entities (Government, Unified System, Banks) to ensure operational availability and monitor interactions.
